item.title: Cunda, you should assemble together in harmony and mutual respect, without disputing. A fellow celibate teaches the Dhamma in the midst of the Saṅgha. If…
item.description:
Cunda, you should assemble together in harmony and mutual respect, without disputing. A fellow celibate teaches the Dhamma in the midst of the Saṅgha. If you think: 'This venerable one has grasped the meaning incorrectly or the phrasing incorrectly,' you should neither praise nor reject him, but should carefully explain to him both the meaning and the phrasing.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 29 – Pāsādikasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Friends, when a noble disciple understands suffering, understands the origin of suffering, understands the cessation of suffering, understands the path leading to the cessation of…
item.description:
Friends, when a noble disciple understands suffering, understands the origin of suffering, understands the cessation of suffering, understands the path leading to the cessation of suffering — then, friends, that noble disciple has right view, straight view, perfect confidence in the Dhamma, and has attained this excellent Dhamma.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 9 – Sammādiṭṭhisutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, here a bhikkhu goes to the forest, to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut, and sits down, folding his legs…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, here a bhikkhu goes to the forest, to the root of a tree, or to an empty hut, and sits down, folding his legs crosswise, keeping his body straight, and establishing mindfulness in front of him. Mindfully he breathes in, mindfully he breathes out. Breathing in long, he understands: 'I breathe in long'; or breathing out long, he understands: 'I breathe out long'; breathing in short, he understands: 'I breathe in short'; or breathing out short, he understands: 'I breathe out short'; 'Experiencing the whole body, I will breathe in,' he trains; 'Experiencing the whole body, I will breathe out,' he trains; 'Tranquilizing the bodily formation, I will breathe in,' he trains; 'Tranquilizing the bodily formation, I will breathe out,' he trains.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 22 – Mahāsatipaṭṭhānasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: And, bhikkhus, what is the person who dwells in darkness heading toward light? Here, bhikkhus, a person is born into a low family — a…
item.description:
And, bhikkhus, what is the person who dwells in darkness heading toward light? Here, bhikkhus, a person is born into a low family — a scavenger's family, a hunter's family, a basket-maker's family, a bamboo-worker's family, a trapper's family, a cart-maker's family, or a sweeper's family — a poor family with little food and drink, where livelihood is hard, where food and clothing are hard to find. And he is ugly, unsightly, deformed, ill, blind in one eye, crooked-limbed, lame, or half-paralyzed; he does not obtain food, drink, clothing, vehicles, garlands, scents, ointments, beds, dwellings, or lamps. He dwells doing good bodily actions, good verbal actions, good mental actions. Having done so, after death, with the breakup of the body, he is reborn in a good destination, a heavenly world. Thus, bhikkhus, is the person who dwells in darkness heading toward light.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 4.85
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: And, bhikkhus, how does a bhikkhu not know the path? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu does not know as it actually is the Noble Eightfold Path.…
item.description:
And, bhikkhus, how does a bhikkhu not know the path? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu does not know as it actually is the Noble Eightfold Path. Thus, bhikkhus, is a bhikkhu who does not know the path. And, bhikkhus, how is a bhikkhu not skilled in the pastures? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu does not know as it actually is the Four Foundations of Mindfulness. Thus, bhikkhus, is a bhikkhu who is not skilled in the pastures. And, bhikkhus, how does a bhikkhu milk dry? Here, bhikkhus, when the lay followers, out of faith, offer the requisites such as robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines, the bhikkhu does not know how to accept just enough. Thus, bhikkhus, is a bhikkhu who milks dry. And, bhikkhus, how is a bhikkhu one who does not pay special respect and reverence to the elder bhikkhus who are senior, gone forth long ago, the fathers of the Saṅgha, the leaders of the Saṅgha? Here, bhikkhus, towards the elder bhikkhus who are senior, gone forth long ago, the fathers of the Saṅgha, the leaders of the Saṅgha, he does not maintain bodily acts of loving-kindness in private and in public; he does not maintain verbal acts of loving-kindness... mental acts of loving-kindness in private and in public. Thus, bhikkhus, is a bhikkhu who does not pay special respect and reverence to the elder bhikkhus. Bhikkhus, a bhikkhu who lacks these eleven qualities cannot grow, prosper, and develop in this Dhamma and Discipline.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 33 – Mahāgopālakasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Furthermore, Nigrodha, an ascetic sees another samaṇa or brahmin being honored, respected, revered, and offered to by families. Seeing that, he thinks: 'These families honor,…
item.description:
Furthermore, Nigrodha, an ascetic sees another samaṇa or brahmin being honored, respected, revered, and offered to by families. Seeing that, he thinks: 'These families honor, respect, revere, and offer to this person who eats all kinds of things. They do not honor, respect, revere, or offer to me, an ascetic practicing austerities.' Thus he becomes jealous and envious of those families. Nigrodha, this is a defilement for an ascetic.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 25 – Udumbarika­sīhanādasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: - Bhikkhus, just as the dawn is the forerunner and precursor of the rising of the sun, so too, bhikkhus, the forerunner and precursor of…
item.description:
- Bhikkhus, just as the dawn is the forerunner and precursor of the rising of the sun, so too, bhikkhus, the forerunner and precursor of the arising of the Noble Eightfold Path is good friendship. Bhikkhus, for a bhikkhu with good friends, it can be expected that the Noble Eightfold Path will be developed, that the Noble Eightfold Path will be cultivated. And how, bhikkhus, does a bhikkhu with good friends develop the Noble Eightfold Path and cultivate the Noble Eightfold Path? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu develops right view connected with seclusion, connected with dispassion, connected with cessation, maturing in relinquishment. He develops right intention ... right concentration connected with seclusion, connected with dispassion, connected with cessation, maturing in relinquishment. Thus, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu with good friends develops the Noble Eightfold Path and cultivates the Noble Eightfold Path.

From: Saṃyutta Nikāya 45.50-54
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, this is the ultimate livelihood among livelihoods, namely, going for alms. In the world, the abusive term is: 'You are a beggar, carrying a…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, this is the ultimate livelihood among livelihoods, namely, going for alms. In the world, the abusive term is: 'You are a beggar, carrying a bowl, wandering everywhere.' Yet this livelihood is accepted by good clansmen who live for the sake of the goal, who live dependent on the goal, not driven to it by fear of kings, fear of thieves, by debt, by fear, not because of loss of livelihood, but thinking: 'I am fallen into birth, aging, death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief, and despair, fallen into suffering, overcome by suffering. Perhaps the complete destruction of this whole mass of suffering can be made known.' Bhikkhus, such a clansman who has gone forth, if he still has craving for sensual pleasures, strong greed, a hateful mind, evil thoughts, forgetfulness, lack of clear comprehension, unconcentrated, with distracted mind and loose faculties — then, bhikkhus, like a funeral torch taken from a cremation ground, burning at both ends, smeared with excrement in the middle, not serving as a log in the village or in the forest — I say that such a person is like that simile. Though he has left household wealth, he does not fulfill the goal of asceticism.

From: Itivuttaka 91
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Therefore, Cunda, the robe that I have allowed you to wear is sufficient for you to ward off cold, to ward off heat, to ward…
item.description:
Therefore, Cunda, the robe that I have allowed you to wear is sufficient for you to ward off cold, to ward off heat, to ward off the touch of flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, and creeping things, and to conceal your private parts.
The almsfood that I have allowed you to use is sufficient to nourish the body, to sustain the body, to avoid harm, and to support the holy life, with the hope: 'Thus I shall destroy old feelings and not arouse new feelings. So my life will be blameless and I shall dwell at ease.'

From: Dīgha Nikāya 29 – Pāsādikasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, in the future there will be bhikkhus who live in association with gardeners and with novice monks. When living in association with gardeners and…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, in the future there will be bhikkhus who live in association with gardeners and with novice monks. When living in association with gardeners and novice monks, bhikkhus, it is to be expected that they will live enjoying stored-up goods, and they will make rough signs regarding land and agricultural products. This, bhikkhus, is the fifth fear regarding the future, not yet arisen now but will arise in the future. You should fully understand them. Having fully understood them, you should strive to abandon that fear.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 5.80
📖 Read this sutta
